input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
    Title="MyMauiApp"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard — single page, shown as its own flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products — flyout item with two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings — separate flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to create an AppShell.xaml for a .NET MAUI app with a specific structure. Let me provide the code directly since this is a straightforward request for a code example.

Structure needed:
1. Flyout with:
   - "Dashboard" section (single page)
   - "Products" section with two tabs: "Active" and "Archived"
   - "Settings" page
2. Route registration for ProductDetailsPage

Let me create the AppShell.xaml and AppShell.xaml.cs files.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
    Title="MyMauiApp"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard — single page, shown as its own flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products — flyout item with two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings — separate flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.content:
Here's the breakdown:

**AppShell.xaml**
- **Dashboard** → a `FlyoutItem` with a single `ShellContent` (no tabs).
- **Products** → a `FlyoutItem` containing two `<Tab>` elements ("Active" and "Archived"), which render as bottom tabs on mobile.
- **Settings** → its own `FlyoutItem`.

**AppShell.xaml.cs**
- Registers `ProductDetailsPage` as a global route (`"productdetails"`). Since it's not part of the Shell hierarchy, it must be registered via `Routing.RegisterRoute`.

**Navigating to ProductDetailsPage** from any products list:

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"productdetails?id={product.Id}");
```

And receive the parameter in `ProductDetailsPage`:

```csharp
[QueryProperty(nameof(ProductId), "id")]
public partial class ProductDetailsPage : ContentPage
{
    public string ProductId { get; set; }
}
```

You'll need to create the five page classes under a `Pages/` namespace: `DashboardPage`, `ActiveProductsPage`, `ArchivedProductsPage`, `SettingsPage`, and `ProductDetailsPage`.
